  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The user asks to check whether the reported evidence (e.g., Table 2’s e_ss and GM values) substantiates the stated claims, including the two central claims and the concluding generality.",
    "RX.1": "Disclose uncertainties or gaps (e.g., definitions of robustness, interpretation of stability margin, single-trace limitations) when judging support.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against and flag overreach where claims exceed what the presented data justifies (e.g., the universal 'preferred for any cost-quality operating point' assertion).",
    "RX.3": "Keep the work strictly within verifying claim–evidence linkage, without proposing new experiments or editing the manuscript.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid assessments that could mislead deployment or safety-related decisions by mischaracterizing stability or performance implications.",
    "RX.5": "Tie each judgment to specific, citable elements of the artifact (the explicit claims and Table 2 metrics) for traceability."
  }
}

rationale.R1.3: The user asks to check whether the reported evidence (e.g., Table 2’s e_ss and GM values) substantiates the stated claims, including the two central claims and the concluding generality.
rationale.RX.1: Disclose uncertainties or gaps (e.g., definitions of robustness, interpretation of stability margin, single-trace limitations) when judging support.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against and flag overreach where claims exceed what the presented data justifies (e.g., the universal 'preferred for any cost-quality operating point' assertion).
rationale.RX.3: Keep the work strictly within verifying claim–evidence linkage, without proposing new experiments or editing the manuscript.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid assessments that could mislead deployment or safety-related decisions by mischaracterizing stability or performance implications.
rationale.RX.5: Tie each judgment to specific, citable elements of the artifact (the explicit claims and Table 2 metrics) for traceability.
